All right, building your foundation. Okay. What is the foundation of your company? The foundation is, so I want to talk about what a client-centered company is. A client centricity is not just about offering great customer experience from the first stage of awareness through the sales cycle and finally, and so it's the concept that's based on putting your cuss that you run a client-centered company, meaning your mission is based and results is what will keep your business alive and is built to serve the customer that is just a fundamental belief I have and I suggest you run it the same way. So what is your client-centric mission statement? Okay, it's very simple. It's just a written declaration of an organization's core purpose and focus for the most parts that remain. I want you to think what things will never change. You could service different people. It could change. It could go older, younger, kids, female. Your team understands that. So what will properly craft statements actually do? Okay, they're going to do three things. One, they're going to serve us filters to separate what is it by making a large strategic decision of the business. Right? Are we going to roll out this new product? Are we going to roll out this new program? I'm going to look at my client-centered mission and be like, does it align with the mission? Will it bring the industry from its knees to its feet? We clearly state the avatar that you're serving and just getting them results physically to enhance their performance at work with their relationships. What is that your end goal? This. Okay, and three, it communicates a sense of intended direction towards the entire company that there's a long-term vision in my company, whether it be a small, and I'm telling you when I say small, I mean it could be anywhere from, right? But most people want to join barge 3000 to 5000 plus organizations. So what advantage do you have over the other large box gym? Then, who do you want to help? Okay, so this bar right there. Okay. Two, what is the change I want to make by helping them? What would their life look like? What is the result of being, that's what you write down there. Third, how will I help them? So how, which is the how and the what, are we going to get them to this goal? Is it by classes? Is it by teaching them about their marriage, about their finances? What things can you do if you're trying to help there? What can you go above and beyond to do to help them to get to that goal, right? And lastly, is how will I know when we are successful? So what is the measurement of that? Have you helped a certain amount of people? Have you had a certain amount of testimonials? Have you been ranked as number one gym in your area? What is that measurement, right? And some of it will be hard, like numbers, and some of it will be soft, like concepts that you just have to go by feel. And that's okay.
